revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishinukinagi Yokoanabo Cluster]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos | native_name =石貫ナギノ横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|58|03|N|130|33|53|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|58|03|N|130|33|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos'''|石貫ナギノ横穴群|Ishinukinagi no yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing 45 [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Yokoanabo|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in the Ishinuki neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was granted protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217779|title=石貫ナギノ横穴群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 December 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ishinukinagi Yokoanabos are located about 5 kilometers north from the urban center of Tamana on a seven-meter cliff face of {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} that continues on the right bank of the middlereaches of the Hanegi River for approximately 250 meters. There are 45 cave tombs on the eastern side of the cliff, clustered into groups of as many as 14 and as few as 3, and they vary in size. Along with neighboring [[Ishinuki Anakannon Yokoanabos]], they reflect the continental characteristics of the area after the introduction of Buddhism into Japan. The highest tombs are located 5 meters above ground, and the lower ones are mostly buried underground. Most of the entrances have double or triple arches or are trapezoidal with rounded corners. Inside, continuous patterns such as concentric circles, triangles, circles, and diamonds are engraved into the walls and are painted with red pigment. Some of the interior and exterior walls of the stone-roofed tomb at the back of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are engraved with bows, arrows, and shields and are thought to be the tombs of powerful clans that held sway in this region at the end of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site si approximately 3.7 kilometers north of {{ill|Shin-Tamana Station|en|Shin-Tamana Station|qid=Q1136695|lt=Shin-Tamana Station|1=Shin-Tamana Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|qid=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.tamana.lg.jp/q/aview/331/16289.html Tamana City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
